title: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3
display_title: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3
wikitext:
[]: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sun Nov 15, 2020 4:27 am Looking at trailer 3, the last two action shots which feature the Wunder in combat. However in the second last shot, a AAA ship that is firing has two noticeable differences. The three large communication dishes are gone and are replaced by diamond like structures. Also the lining around the beak is blue instead of red. Cut to the next shot showing the obvious AAA Wunder flying towards the camera and taking firing the diferences become all too obvious. This has been speculated before that Nerv/Seele also have a AAA ship based on the bridge scene near the end of 3.0. It would explain how Nerv escaped HQ and recovered Unit 13.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun Nov 15, 2020 7:54 am Pretty much "yes".

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Tajai » Sun Nov 15, 2020 11:16 am wiser3754 wrote: Looking at trailer 3, the last two action shots which feature the Wunder in combat. However in the second last shot, a AAA ship that is firing has two noticeable differences. The three large communication dishes are gone and are replaced by diamond like structures. Also the lining around the beak is blue instead of red. Cut to the next shot showing the obvious AAA Wunder flying towards the camera and taking firing the diferences become all too obvious. This has been speculated before that Nerv/Seele also have a AAA ship based on the bridge scene near the end of 3.0. It would explain how Nerv escaped HQ and recovered Unit 13.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Nov 15, 2020 12:33 pm On an off note, there has also been some speculation about the possible name of the new ship - the one I like the most is Unheil , German for disaster/calamity

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Nov 15, 2020 12:59 pm ^ While I know NTE is much more Anime than NGE ever was, but Gendou and Fuyutsuki being so cartoonish as to pettishly actively bringing Unheil upon the world is kinda tacky, imo.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Nov 15, 2020 4:37 pm I'm thinking more along the lines of having it be in contrast to the Wunder, rather than it being necessarily reflecting of Gendo and Fuyutsuki's goals

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Mr. M » Sun Nov 15, 2020 4:59 pm I'd like to know the origins of these ship tho, like how they were concieved (or found?)in the first place.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by intermediateO » Sun Nov 15, 2020 10:09 pm Mr. M wrote: I'd like to know the origins of these ship tho, like how they were concieved (or found?)in the first place.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Tue Nov 17, 2020 5:30 pm Okay so we're agreed. There are two Arks. Nerv pratically gave Wille both the Wunder and Unit 01 to power it ensuring they'll bring Unit 01 to the final showdown. So here's the next question. What is powering Nerv's ark? Is it Unit 13 or the Mark.10?

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by intermediateO » Tue Nov 17, 2020 6:23 pm wiser3754 wrote: So here's the next question. What is powering Nerv's ark? Is it Unit 13 or the Mark.10?

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Nov 18, 2020 4:00 am Alternatively: It's the same one after some repairs or transformations. Are both ever in the same shot?

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Nov 18, 2020 6:59 am ^ They're not in the same shot, but the scene as it appears in the teaser feels like a “Shot-Reverse-Shot” edit of two different vessels. I rarely question the use of that technique, especially in trailers or teasers. There’s just not enough information in the teaser in order to make a judgement call on it.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Fredsq » Sat Nov 28, 2020 2:01 pm I don't think the arks "are" anything in particular: they're supersized evangelion units (i.e. a clone of Adam or Lilith) build by Wille, with a different composition, not mimicking the human shape but something functional. We see evangelions (Mk.04 and friends) diverging more and more from the chest-two-upper-two-lower-limb composition. Mk. 4444C has several torsos and tentacles for legs, why couldn't they have build a massive thing such as the Wunder? My theory is: Wunder is a copy of one of the Adams (the same one Mark 09 is made of) in a huge body, and is designed to be activated by different means. We've seen plenty of times how angels use their AT Field to propel them upwards, in order to fly (Sachiel, Zeruel, Mk. 06, Evangelion 01…) and this is how the Wunder was able to harness Eva-01's AT Field to fly. Wunder is like an Evangelion for Evangelions, where Eva-01 is "the pilot". This has even led me to imagine that Wille has in fact harnessed Rei's soul from Eva-01 and are using it at the core of the Wunder, to establish synchronisation between Eva 01 and the Wunder! I have no idea of how Nerv would manage to get another ark, but seeing as they made Eva-13 from scratch and Shini's had no problems syncing with it albeit Wille clearly showing his Sync Rate is 0%, it may mean they have found a soul replacement. Sorry, as a lurker I had a lot to say

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Mon Nov 30, 2020 5:38 pm The Wunder was built by Wille? Yet Nerv's or Seele's Mark.09 was it's original master? I think Wille commandeered the Wunder after Seele surrendered it leaving Nerv with it's 'Nemesis' ark. Don't forget the actions of Colonel Katsuragi during the 4th impact was part of Gendo's plan as was Wille's operation to secure Unit 01. I wouldn't be surprised Nerv tipped off Wille about the tesseract's location. Wille has nothing that Nerv/Seele willingly allows them to have.

[]: Re: Are there two AAA ships in trailer 3?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 18, 2020 11:28 am To me it seems pretty apparent that Misato stole it , and fairly recently at that. Just from how the design resembles Mark 9 resembles it's bridge, the animal noises, the "original master" line, it seems heavily implied that it was something Neo Nerv built to be controlled by Mark 9 or something like it. EVA 01 was then procured as an alternate power source. Indeed if you look closely they've used a 44A as a power source (presumably, Asuka and Mari caught it)- see the analysis wiki article on the wiki, some good catches there ) but that's not enough for flying. From the dialogue it seems implies that they haven't had it for long, it was as of yet rather untested, they're only just transferring their personnel there... Also there's it's resemblance to the Third angel with all the bones, and how it was apparently tried to affix an entry plug & a power cable to it... The Wunder and hypothetical sister ships might be copies of it. That#s something I love about EVA that it does tech so realistically. Like you see tests & jury rigged versions of something first then it goes into mass production, then improved versions... The tech over the timeskip had improved, the interface is now 360 degrees, the plugsuits thinner & smoother, the batteries better etc. I kinda wish we'd seen her steal it. Well, ultimately it would just be an action scene that wouldn't add more to the plot than having it implied that that's what they did, but I bet it was cool.I bet she recruited an army of downtrodden youngsters from the remaining refugee camps, whipped them into shape & got them to believe in hope. Having Misato & co fight a "sister ship" (if that is not just a mod after all) might of course be the excuse for all about it to be explained/ come up in dialogue. I feel a bit reminded of the Vector Craft from the Evangelion Manga, "Were basically in a giant angel here" oThe title "Ark" might just be a flourish/flavor/symbolic, but it's fully possible that it has some impact related powers/properties. Or maybe it just somehow (AT Field?) protects the people inside and nearbyfrom core-ization so they can move more freely than they otherwise would, like the original arc protects the near extinct life from the flood.
